Your Angular app is not slow because of Angular.
It is slow because of you.
I said the same thing to myself eighteen months ago, staring at a change detection cycle that ran four hundred times for a single button click. Four hundred. For one click.
I fixed three habits. Just three.
The app got faster. The code reviews got shorter. And a senior title followed, not because I asked for it, but because the code stopped looking junior.
This is not a theory article. Every snippet below is something I broke, watched fail in production, and rebuilt. Every number is from a real measurement, not a marketing slide.
Grab your coffee. Let us get into it.
Habit 1: They Stop Fighting Change Detection, They Design Around It
The problem
Most junior Angular code treats change detection like background noise. It is not. It is the engine room of your app, and if you never open the door, you never find out why the ship is slow.
Here is a component that looks completely normal. Thousands of teams ship code exactly like this every day.
@Component({…
